info.title: CloudCDN API
info.description:
# CloudCDN API

Enterprise-grade asset delivery on Cloudflare's global edge network. 300+ PoPs, sub-100ms TTFB, 1,400+ assets across 54 tenant zones.

## Quickstart

```bash
# List all assets
curl -H 'AccessKey: YOUR_KEY' https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets

# Upload a file
curl -X PUT -H 'AccessKey: YOUR_KEY' \
  -T logo.svg \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/clients/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg

# Transform an image
curl 'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/transform?url=/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg&w=400&format=webp'
```

## Authentication

CloudCDN uses two separate API keys to isolate data-plane and control-plane access:

| Key | Header | Scope |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **AccessKey** | `AccessKey` | Storage, Assets — upload, download, browse |
| **AccountKey** | `AccountKey` | Core, Insights — zones, domains, rules, analytics |

Pass the key in the request header:

```bash
curl -H 'AccessKey: sk_live_abc123' https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets
```

Public endpoints (Transform, Auto-format, Search, Chat) require no authentication.

## Base URL

| Environment | URL |
|-------------|-----|
| Production | `https://cloudcdn.pro` |
| Local Dev | `http://localhost:8788` |

## Client Libraries

Pre-built client libraries for every endpoint. Copy-paste ready.

### JavaScript (ES Module)

```javascript
import { listAssets, storageUpload, transformImage } from './clients/javascript.js';

// List all SVG assets
const result = await listAssets({ format: 'svg', per_page: 50 });
console.log(result.Data);

// Upload a file
await storageUpload('clients/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg', fileBuffer);

// Transform on the fly
const img = await transformImage({ url: '/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg', w: 400, format: 'webp' });
```

[Download javascript.js](/api-reference/clients/javascript.js)

### TypeScript

```typescript
import { listAssets, type ListAssetsParams } from './clients/typescript';

const params: ListAssetsParams = { project: 'akande', format: 'svg' };
const result = await listAssets(params);
```

[Download typescript.ts](/api-reference/clients/typescript.ts)

### Python

```python
from clients.python import list_assets, storage_upload

assets = list_assets(project='akande', format='svg')
for asset in assets['Data']:
    print(asset['Path'])
```

[Download python.py](/api-reference/clients/python.py)

### cURL

```bash
# List all SVG assets
curl -s -H 'AccessKey: sk_live_abc123' \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets?format=svg&per_page=50'

# Upload a file
curl -X PUT -H 'AccessKey: sk_live_abc123' \
  -H 'Content-Type: image/svg+xml' \
  -T ./logo.svg \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/clients/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg'

# Transform an image (public — no key needed)
curl -o thumbnail.webp \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/transform?url=/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg&w=400&format=webp'

# Create a zone
curl -X POST -H 'AccountKey: ak_live_xyz789' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"Name": "newclient"}' \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/core/zones'

# Purge cache by tag
curl -X POST -H 'x-api-key: pk_live_purge' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"tags": ["project-akande"]}' \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/purge'
```

### Shell Helper

Source the shell client for reusable functions:

```bash
source clients/curl.sh
export CLOUDCDN_ACCESS_KEY='sk_live_abc123'
listAssets   # calls GET /api/assets
```

[Download curl.sh](/api-reference/clients/curl.sh)

## Rate Limits

| Endpoint | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| Image Transform | 50,000/month |
| Cache Purge | 100/day |
| Chat Concierge | 1,000/month |
| Storage Upload | No hard limit (GitHub API rate applies) |
| Asset Catalog | No limit (manifest cached 30s) |

## API Planes

| Plane | Prefix | Purpose |
|-------|--------|---------|
| **Storage** | `/api/storage/` | File upload, download, delete, batch |
| **Core** | `/api/core/` | Zones, domains, rules, statistics |
| **Assets** | `/api/assets` | Read-only catalog, metadata |
| **Insights** | `/api/insights/` | Analytics, top assets, geo, errors |
| **Delivery** | `/api/transform`, `/api/auto`, `/api/signed`, `/api/stream`, `/api/purge` | Edge transforms, format negotiation, signed URLs, HLS, cache |
| **AI** | `/api/search`, `/api/chat` | Semantic search, RAG concierge |

tags.name: Storage
tags.description:
## Storage API

Upload, download, and manage files across your CDN. The Storage API is the write layer — every file mutation flows through here.

### How it works

1. **Upload** a file with `PUT /api/storage/{path}` — committed to the repository via the GitHub Git API
2. **The CI/CD pipeline** deploys it to 300+ edge locations (~60-90 seconds)
3. **Download** it instantly via `GET /api/storage/{path}` or the public CDN URL

### Quick start

```bash
# Upload an SVG logo
curl -X PUT -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: image/svg+xml" \
  -T ./logo.svg \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/clients/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg

# List a directory
curl -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/clients/myproject/v1/logos/

# Batch upload (single atomic commit)
curl -X POST -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "files": [{ "path": "clients/myproject/v1/logos/logo.svg", "content": "PHN2Zy4uLg==", "encoding": "base64" }] }' \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/batch
```

### Authentication

All Storage endpoints require an **AccessKey** header. This key grants read/write access to files but cannot modify zones or edge configuration.

### Limits

| Limit | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Max file size | 25 MB |
| Max batch size | 50 files |
| Checksum verification | SHA-256 (optional) |
| Edge sync | ~60-90 seconds after commit |

tags.name: Assets
tags.description:
## Assets API

Browse and search your entire asset catalog. The Assets API is the read-only layer — it queries the auto-generated manifest for instant, filterable access to every file on your CDN.

### How it works

Every time a file changes, the manifest regenerates automatically. The Assets API reads this manifest (cached 30 seconds at the edge) and provides:

- **Paginated listing** — filter by project, category, format
- **Full-text search** — across filenames and paths
- **Per-asset metadata** — size, content type, CDN URL, available formats

### Quick start

```bash
# List all SVG assets sorted by size
curl -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets?format=svg&sort=size&order=desc'

# Search for banking logos
curl -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets?q=banking+logo'

# Get metadata for a specific asset
curl -H "AccessKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets/metadata?path=akande/v1/logos/akande.svg'
```

### Pagination

Results paginate with `page` and `per_page` (max 200). The response includes `Pagination.TotalItems`, `Pagination.TotalPages`, and the current page.

tags.name: Core
tags.description:
## Core API

Manage your CDN infrastructure — zones, domains, edge rules, and statistics. The Core API is the control plane for platform administrators.

### Zones

A **zone** is a tenant namespace (e.g., `akande`, `bankingonai`). Each zone maps to a directory with a standardized structure:

```
clients/{zone}/v1/
  banners/    — Hero images and promotional banners
  github/     — Social card images for GitHub
  icons/      — PWA icons (180, 192, 512)
  logos/       — Brand logos (SVG source)
  titles/      — Title card images
```

### Quick start

```bash
# Create a new zone
curl -X POST -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  -d '{ "Name": "newclient" }' \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/core/zones

# Add a custom domain
curl -X POST -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  -d '{ "Hostname": "cdn.newclient.com" }' \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/core/zones/newclient/domains

# Update edge caching rules
curl -X POST -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  -d '{ "File": "_headers", "Content": "/*.webp\n  Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400" }' \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/core/rules
```

### Authentication

All Core endpoints require an **AccountKey** header. This is the admin key — it can create/delete zones, manage domains, and modify edge rules. Never share it with CI/CD bots.

### Reserved zone names

These names cannot be used: `stocks`, `shared`, `website`, `functions`, `api`, `dashboard`, `dist`, `content`, `global`, `clients`.

tags.name: Insights
tags.description:
## Insights API

Monitor your CDN performance in real time. The Insights API provides analytics data collected by the edge middleware on every asset request.

### What is tracked

| Metric | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Requests** | Total hits per day |
| **Bandwidth** | Estimated bytes served |
| **Cache ratio** | Edge hits vs origin misses |
| **Geography** | Requests by country code |
| **Top assets** | Most requested files |
| **Errors** | 4xx/5xx breakdown with paths |

### Quick start

```bash
# 7-day summary
curl -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/insights/summary?days=7

# Top 20 assets this month
curl -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/insights/top-assets?days=30&limit=20

# Geographic distribution
curl -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/insights/geography?days=7

# Error tracking
curl -H "AccountKey: YOUR_KEY" \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/insights/errors?days=7
```

### Per-zone filtering

Add `?zone=akande` to any endpoint to filter analytics for a specific tenant.

### Authentication

Insights accept either **AccountKey** or **AccessKey** — read-only and safe for dashboards.

tags.name: Delivery
tags.description:
## Delivery API

Transform, optimize, and deliver assets at the edge. These endpoints run on Cloudflare's global network — results are cached at 300+ locations worldwide.

### Image Transformation

Resize, convert, blur, and sharpen any image on the fly:

```
https://cloudcdn.pro/api/transform?url=/akande/v1/logos/akande.svg&w=400&format=webp&q=85
```

| Parameter | Range | Description |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| `w` | 1–8192 | Width in pixels |
| `h` | 1–8192 | Height in pixels |
| `fit` | cover, contain, fill | Resize behavior |
| `format` | webp, avif, png, jpeg, auto | Output format |
| `q` | 1–100 | Quality (lower = smaller file) |
| `blur` | 1–250 | Gaussian blur radius |
| `sharpen` | 1–10 | Sharpening amount |
| `gravity` | center, face, auto | Crop focus point |

### Automatic Format Negotiation

Request any image without a format — the edge reads the browser's `Accept` header and serves the optimal format (AVIF > WebP > PNG):

```
https://cloudcdn.pro/api/auto?path=/akande/v1/logos/akande
```

### Signed URLs

Protect premium assets with time-limited, HMAC-signed URLs that expire automatically.

### Video Streaming (HLS)

Adaptive bitrate video via HTTP Live Streaming playlists.

### Cache Purge

Instantly invalidate assets by URL, surrogate tag, or purge everything.

### No authentication required

All Delivery endpoints are public — they are the URLs your end users hit directly. Rate limits prevent abuse (50,000 transforms/month, 100 purges/day).

tags.name: AI
tags.description:
## AI Services

Search and explore your asset catalog using natural language. Powered by Cloudflare Workers AI and Vectorize.

### Semantic Search

Find assets by description, not filename:

```bash
# Find dark banking backgrounds
curl 'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/search?q=dark+blue+banking+background'

# Find minimalist logos
curl 'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/search?q=minimalist+logo+svg'
```

The engine uses a hybrid approach: vector similarity (Vectorize embeddings) with fuzzy text fallback. Results are scored and ranked by relevance.

### AI Concierge

An intelligent chat assistant that knows your entire CDN — pricing, setup, performance, compliance, and troubleshooting. Powered by RAG over the knowledge base.

```bash
curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "message": "How do I set up custom domains?", "history": [] }' \
  https://cloudcdn.pro/api/chat
```

### No authentication required

AI endpoints are public. Rate limits: 1,000 chat queries/month, 100 search requests/minute.

AccountKey.name: AccountKey
AccountKey.description: Administrative API key for core management endpoints (zones, rules, statistics). Set via the ACCOUNT_KEY environment variable.

AccessKey.name: AccessKey
AccessKey.description: Storage and asset access key. Set via the STORAGE_KEY environment variable. Also accepted on Insights endpoints.

PurgeKey.name: x-api-key
PurgeKey.description: Purge endpoint API key. Set via the PURGE_KEY environment variable.

AnalyticsKey.name: x-api-key
AnalyticsKey.description: Analytics reporting API key. Set via the ANALYTICS_KEY environment variable.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response following Microsoft API Guidelines and RFC 9457. Includes machine-readable error code, human-readable message, request tracing, and optional inner error details.

error.description: Error object containing structured error details.

code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling.

message.description: Human-readable error description with remediation guidance (150+ characters).
message.example: The request body contains invalid JSON and could not be parsed. Verify the payload is well-formed JSON with correct syntax.

target.description: The target of the error (e.g., the field name that caused the error).

details.description: Additional error details for validation errors.

innererror.description: Internal error details for debugging (only in non-production).

Message.description: Human-readable error message (duplicate of error.message for backward compatibility).

requestId.description: Unique request identifier for support and debugging.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the error occurred.

apiVersion.description: API version that processed the request.

JsonErrorResponse.description: Simplified JSON error response used by public-facing endpoints (transform, auto, search, chat, purge) that return a single error string.

Pagination.description: Pagination metadata included in paginated list responses. Provides current page, page size, total item count, and total page count for client-side navigation.

TotalItems.description: Total number of items matching the query across all pages

AssetEntry.description: A single asset entry in the catalog. Represents a file stored on the CDN with its name, full path, owning project, category classification, file format, and byte size.

path.description: Full path within the CDN asset tree

AssetMetadata.description: Detailed metadata for a single asset, including CDN delivery URL, transform URL, available format variants, content type, and human-readable size. Used by the asset detail endpoint.

Path.description: Full asset path within the CDN tree

CdnUrl.description: Direct CDN delivery URL for this asset

TransformUrl.description: URL to the transform endpoint pre-populated with this asset

AvailableFormats.description: List of format variants available for this asset

StorageFileEntry.description: A file or directory entry in the storage system. Uses the Bunny.net-compatible format with GUID, storage zone info, path, size, timestamps, and directory flag for recursive listing.

Guid.description: Unique identifier for this storage entry

StorageZoneName.description: Name of the storage zone containing this entry

Path.description: Directory path within the storage zone (always ends with /)

Length.description: File size in bytes (0 for directories)

ServerId.description: Internal server identifier (always 0 for GitOps storage)

ArrayNumber.description: Internal array number (always 0 for GitOps storage)

IsDirectory.description: Whether this entry represents a directory rather than a file

ContentType.description: MIME content type (empty string for directories)

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the file was first created

StorageZoneId.description: Numeric storage zone identifier (always 0 for GitOps storage)

Checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum of the file contents, or null if not computed

ReplicatedZones.description: Comma-separated list of replication zones (empty for single-region)

UploadResponse.description: Response returned after a successful file upload. Contains the committed file path, size, optional checksum, creation timestamp, and edge deployment status.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (201 for successful upload)

Path.description: Committed file path within the storage tree

Checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum if provided in the request, otherwise null

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the file was committed

EdgeStatus.description: Edge deployment status: pending (new file) or purging (overwrite triggering cache invalidation)

EdgeNote.description: Informational note about edge availability timeline
EdgeNote.example: File committed to repository. It will be available at the edge after the CI/CD pipeline deploys (~60-90 seconds).

DeleteResponse.description: Response returned after a successful file deletion. Includes the removed file path and edge cache purge status.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (200 for successful deletion)

EdgeStatus.description: Edge cache purge status (always purging for deletes)

EdgeNote.description: Informational note about edge cache purge timeline
EdgeNote.example: File removed from repository and edge cache purge initiated.

BatchUploadRequest.description: Request body for batch file upload. Contains an array of up to 50 files, each with a destination path, content (base64 or UTF-8 encoded), and optional encoding specification.

files.description: Array of files to upload in a single atomic Git commit

BatchUploadResponse.description: Response returned after a successful batch upload. Contains the Git commit SHA, list of committed file paths, and edge deployment status for the entire batch.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (201 for successful batch upload)

Message.description: Human-readable success message with file count
Message.example: 3 file(s) uploaded in a single commit

Commit.description: Git commit SHA for the batch operation

Files.description: List of file paths that were committed

EdgeStatus.description: Edge deployment status for the batch

EdgeNote.description: Informational note about edge availability timeline
EdgeNote.example: Files committed. Available at the edge after CI/CD deploy (~60-90 seconds).

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the batch commit was created

Zone.description: Summary representation of a tenant zone (client project). Contains zone identity, storage zone binding, origin URL, aggregate file count and storage usage, asset categories, and operational status.

Id.description: Unique zone identifier (same as the project name)

StorageZoneName.description: Name of the backing storage zone

OriginUrl.description: CDN origin URL for this zone

FileCount.description: Total number of files in the zone

Categories.description: Distinct asset categories present in the zone

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the zone was created

Enabled.description: Whether the zone is currently enabled for serving

Suspended.description: Whether the zone has been suspended (e.g., for abuse)

ZoneDetail.description: Detailed zone representation including the full file listing, available formats, and all categories. Extends the Zone summary with per-file metadata for administrative use.

StorageZoneName.description: Name of the backing storage zone

OriginUrl.description: CDN origin URL for this zone

FileCount.description: Total number of files in the zone

Categories.description: Distinct asset categories present in the zone

Formats.description: Distinct file formats present in the zone

Files.description: Complete list of files in the zone with per-file metadata

items.description: Individual file entry within a zone detail listing

Path.description: Full file path within the CDN tree

Enabled.description: Whether the zone is currently enabled for serving

Suspended.description: Whether the zone has been suspended

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the zone was created

ZoneCreateRequest.description: Request body for creating a new tenant zone. The zone name must be 2-64 lowercase alphanumeric characters with hyphens, and must not already exist.

Name.description: Zone name: 2-64 alphanumeric characters with hyphens

ZoneCreateResponse.description: Response returned after a successful zone creation. Includes the Git commit SHA, scaffolded directories, and edge deployment status.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (201 for successful creation)

OriginUrl.description: CDN origin URL for the new zone

Commit.description: Git commit SHA for the zone creation

Directories.description: Standard v1/ directories scaffolded for the new zone

EdgeNote.description: Informational note about edge availability timeline
EdgeNote.example: Zone will be live after CI/CD deploy (~60-90 seconds).

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the zone was created

ZoneDeleteResponse.description: Response returned after a successful zone deletion. Includes the Git commit SHA, number of files removed, and edge cache purge status.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (200 for successful deletion)

Message.description: Human-readable success message with file removal count

Commit.description: Git commit SHA for the deletion

FilesRemoved.description: Number of files removed in the deletion

EdgeStatus.description: Edge cache purge status (always purging for zone deletes)

DomainAddRequest.description: Request body for adding a custom domain to a zone. The hostname must be a fully qualified domain name with a CNAME record pointing to cloudcdn-pro.pages.dev.

DomainAddResponse.description: Response returned after successfully adding a custom domain to a zone. Includes SSL provisioning status and CNAME configuration instructions.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (201 for successful domain addition)

Zone.description: Zone the domain was added to

Hostname.description: The custom domain hostname that was added

Note.example: SSL certificate will be provisioned automatically. Point your CNAME to cloudcdn-pro.pages.dev.

DateCreated.description: Timestamp when the domain was added

StatisticsResponse.description: Comprehensive edge statistics response including request counts, bandwidth, cache ratios, top assets, and geographic distribution over the specified time period.

Period.description: Time period and scope of the statistics

Days.description: Number of days included in the report

Summary.description: Aggregate summary metrics for the period

CacheHitRate.description: Percentage of requests served from cache

UniqueCountries.description: Number of distinct countries that sent requests

Daily.description: Per-day breakdown of request and bandwidth metrics

items.description: Daily statistics for a single day

Bandwidth.description: Bandwidth in bytes on this day

CacheHit.description: Number of cache hits on this day

CacheMiss.description: Number of cache misses on this day

TopAssets.description: Most-requested assets ranked by request count

items.description: A top asset entry with path and request count

Requests.description: Total request count for this asset

GeoDistribution.description: Request counts by country, sorted descending by volume

items.description: Geographic distribution entry with country code and request count

RulesResponse.description: Current edge rules configuration containing the contents of _headers and _redirects files, the list of editable rule files, and a fetch timestamp.

Editable.description: List of rule files that can be edited

DateFetched.description: Timestamp when the rules were fetched from the repository

RulesUpdateRequest.description: Request body for updating an edge rule file. Specifies which file to update (_headers or _redirects) and the new content (max 100 KB).

File.description: The edge rule file to update

RulesUpdateResponse.description: Response returned after a successful edge rules update. Includes the Git commit SHA and edge deployment status.

HttpCode.description: HTTP status code (200 for successful update)

File.description: Name of the updated rule file

Commit.description: Git commit SHA for the update

EdgeNote.description: Informational note about when changes take effect
EdgeNote.example: Edge rules will take effect after CI/CD deploy (~60-90 seconds).

DateModified.description: Timestamp when the rules were updated

InsightsSummaryResponse.description: Aggregate analytics summary providing total requests, bandwidth, cache hit rate, and unique country count for the specified time period and optional zone filter.

Period.description: Time period and scope of the summary

Days.description: Number of days included in the summary

TotalRequests.description: Total number of requests served during the period

CacheHitRate.description: Percentage of requests served from cache

UniqueCountries.description: Number of distinct countries that sent requests

DateFetched.description: Timestamp when the summary was generated

TopAssetsResponse.description: Response containing the most-requested assets over the specified period, ranked by request count descending.

Days.description: Number of days included in the report

Assets.description: Ranked list of top assets by request count

items.description: A top asset entry with its path and total request count

DateFetched.description: Timestamp when the report was generated

GeographyResponse.description: Geographic distribution of requests over the specified period. Each entry contains an ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code and the request count from that country.

Days.description: Number of days included in the report

Countries.description: Request counts by country, sorted descending by volume

DateFetched.description: Timestamp when the report was generated

ErrorsResponse.description: Error tracking report showing 4xx/5xx error counts grouped by HTTP status code, with the top paths generating each error type over the specified period.

Days.description: Number of days included in the report

Errors.description: Error groups, each containing a status code, total count, and top offending paths

items.description: Error group for a single HTTP status code

Count.description: Total number of errors with this status code

items.description: A path that generated errors with its count

Path.description: Request path that triggered the error

Count.description: Number of errors for this path

Note.description: Informational note when no errors are tracked
Note.example: Error tracking populates automatically from middleware analytics.

DateFetched.description: Timestamp when the report was generated

PurgeRequest.description: Cache purge request. Exactly one of urls, tags, or purge_everything must be specified per request. URLs must start with https://cloudcdn.pro/.

urls.description: Up to 30 URLs to purge. Must start with https://cloudcdn.pro/.

tags.description: Up to 30 cache tags to purge. Alphanumeric with hyphens only.

purge_everything.description: Set to true to purge the entire cache.

PurgeResponse.description: Response returned after a successful cache purge operation. Contains the purged targets, remaining daily purge quota, and the raw Cloudflare API response.

purged.description: The URLs, tags, or "everything" that was purged

cloudflare.description: Raw Cloudflare API response for debugging

AnalyticsReportResponse.description: Daily analytics report with per-day breakdowns of hits, bandwidth, top assets, geographic distribution, and cache ratios. Data is retained in KV for up to 35 days.

days.description: Number of days included in the report

items.description: Analytics data for a single day

hits.description: Total request hits on this day

top_assets.description: Map of asset paths to their request counts for this day

geo.description: Map of country codes to their request counts for this day

cache.description: Cache performance metrics for this day

ratio.description: Cache hit ratio as a percentage string

AnalyticsTrackRequest.description: Request body for recording a single analytics hit. Typically called by edge middleware to increment daily counters for hits, bandwidth, top assets, geo, and cache status.

SearchResponse.description: Semantic search results containing matched assets with relevance scores. Results are ranked by score descending, with vector search yielding scores 0.5-1.0 and fuzzy fallback yielding fractional scores.

results.description: Matched assets ranked by relevance score

items.description: A single search result with asset metadata and relevance score

path.description: Full asset path within the CDN tree

score.description: Relevance score (0-1). Higher from vector search, fractional from fuzzy.

ChatRequest.description: Request body for the AI Chat Concierge. Contains the user's message and optional conversation history (last 5 turns are used for context).

message.example: What image formats does CloudCDN support?

items.description: A single conversation turn with role and content

get.description: Paginated, filterable asset catalog. Streams JSON for sub-2ms TTFB. Supports filtering by project, category, format, and free-text search. Rate limit: none (public with AccessKey).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by asset category (e.g., logos, banners, icons)

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search against name and path (case-insensitive substring match)

400.description: Bad Request — invalid parameters or malformed input.

429.description: Rate Limited — too many requests. Retry after the cooldown period.

500.description: Internal Server Error — unexpected failure at the edge.

example.Message: Asset manifest unavailable. Please try again later.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

res = requests.get(
    'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/assets',
    headers={'AccessKey': 'YOUR_ACCESS_KEY'},
    params={'project': 'akande', 'format': 'svg'}
)
print(res.json()['Data'])

get.description: Returns detailed metadata for a single asset including available format variants, CDN URL, and transform URL.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Full asset path from the manifest

429.description: Rate Limited — too many requests. Retry after the cooldown period.

get.description: If the path ends with `/` or has no file extension, lists directory contents in Bunny.net-compatible JSON. Otherwise, downloads the file. Auth: AccessKey header or dashboard session cookie.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Storage path. Trailing slash = directory listing. File extension = download.

200.description: Directory listing (JSON array) or file body (binary)

example.Message: Invalid path: path traversal not allowed

put.description: Uploads a file to storage via GitHub API commit. Max file size: 25 MB. Supports optional SHA-256 checksum verification via the Checksum header. Triggers async cache purge on overwrites. Rate limit: none (auth-gated).

parameters.name: Checksum
parameters.description: SHA-256 hex digest for integrity verification

example.EdgeNote: File committed to repository. It will be available at the edge after the CI/CD pipeline deploys (~60-90 seconds).

example.Message: Checksum mismatch: expected E3B0C4... got A1B2C3...
Conflict: another upload modified the tree. Retry or use /api/storage/batch for concurrent uploads.
Payload too large. Maximum file size is 25 MB.

429.description: Rate Limited — too many requests. Retry after the cooldown period.

500.description: Internal Server Error — unexpected failure at the edge.

502.description: Bad Gateway — upstream service (GitHub/Cloudflare API) returned an error.

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

with open('logo.svg', 'rb') as f:
    res = requests.put(
        'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/storage/clients/akande/v1/logos/new-logo.svg',
        headers={'AccessKey': 'YOUR_ACCESS_KEY', 'Content-Type': 'application/octet-stream'},
        data=f.read()
    )
print(res.json())

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Python
x-codeSamples.source:
import requests

res = requests.get(
    'https://cloudcdn.pro/api/transform',
    params={'url': '/akande/v1/banners/hero.webp', 'w': 800, 'h': 600, 'fit': 'cover', 'format': 'webp', 'q': 85}
)
with open('transformed.webp', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(res.content)
print(f'Saved {len(res.content)} bytes')

get.description: Serves the best image format based on the client's Accept header. Fallback chain: avif -> webp -> png -> svg. Public endpoint (no auth). Also supports path-based routing: `/api/auto/{path}`. Responses cached for 1 year with Vary: Accept.

200.description: Image in the best supported format. Content-Type header indicates the negotiated format.

Content-Type.description: Negotiated MIME type (image/avif, image/webp, image/png, or image/svg+xml)

404.description: No suitable format found for the given path

example.error: No suitable format found for the given path

get.description: Catch-all route for path-based format negotiation. Delegates to the main auto handler by rewriting the URL path segments into a query parameter. Serves the best image format based on the client's Accept header. Fallback chain: avif -> webp -> png -> svg. Public endpoint (no auth). Responses cached for 1 year with Vary: Accept.

get.description: Pseudo-HLS streaming endpoint. Without quality parameter, returns a master M3U8 playlist. With quality, returns a variant playlist. With quality and segment, returns a byte-range video segment. Public endpoint (no auth). Available videos: black, mount_fuji, nature. Available qualities: 1080, 720, 480. Segment duration: 10 seconds. Segments cached for 1 year.

parameters.name: quality

post.description: Invalidates cached content via the Cloudflare API. Supports three modes (exactly one per request): specific URLs (max 30), cache tags (max 30), or purge everything. Rate limit: 100 purge requests per day. Auth: x-api-key header (PURGE_KEY).

post.description: RAG-powered conversational AI assistant for CloudCDN. Uses Workers AI (Llama 3.1 8B) with Vectorize for context retrieval. Returns a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream with metadata, token, done, and error events. Rate limits: 1,000 queries/month, 100 queries/day (soft). Public endpoint (no auth). Last 5 conversation history turns are used for context.

example.message: What image formats does CloudCDN support?

history.content: We have 3 tiers: Free ($0), Pro ($29/mo), and Enterprise (custom).

200.description: SSE stream with metadata, token, done, and error events

schema.description:
Server-Sent Events stream. Event types:
- `metadata`: `{sources: string[], confidence: 'high'|'medium'|'low', remaining: number|null}`
- `token`: `{text: string}` (individual response tokens)
- `done`: `{followUps: string[]}` (2-3 suggested follow-up questions)
- `error`: `{error: string}`

400.description: Invalid JSON body or missing message

429.description: Monthly query limit reached (1,000/month)

message.example: Monthly query limit reached. The Concierge will be back next month.

example.message: Monthly query limit reached. The Concierge will be back next month.

500.description: Internal error

502.description: Bad Gateway — upstream service (GitHub/Cloudflare API) returned an error.
